title: Aganosma roxburghii
taxonomy: Aganosma roxburghii belongs to the family Apocynaceae, a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. The genus Aganosma is a genus of flowering plants in the family Apocynaceae. The species Aganosma roxburghii is native to the Himalayas and parts of India. It is a woody vine that can be found growing in the wild in shaded areas. The classification of Aganosma roxburghii is further categorized into the kingdom Plantae and the order Gentianales.
uses: Aganosma roxburghii is an evergreen climbing shrub used in traditional medicine.
